title: Classification Error
description: Understand classification error as a simple impurity measure.
takeaways: Classification error is the fraction of misclassified samples in a node.
It is simpler than Gini but less sensitive to impurity changes.
Trees usually prefer Gini or entropy for split quality.

practiceLab.objective: Calculate classification error and compare its sensitivity with Gini and entropy on nearly-pure and balanced nodes.
instructions: Run the helpers cell defining classification_error, gini_impurity, and entropy.
Run the guided [6,4] example and confirm classification error is 0.400.
Run the 9/1 vs 8/2 sensitivity comparison cell.
Note that error changes less than Gini/entropy between those nodes.
Complete Exercise 1 for labels with seven 0s and three 1s (error 0.300).
Complete Exercise 2 printing error/gini/entropy for pure, 7/3, and 5/5.
Confirm pure error is 0.000 and 5/5 error is 0.500.
Answer the reflection questions about sensitivity and split criteria.
hints: Near-pure nodes can have similar error but different Gini/entropy.
A balanced two-class node has error 0.5.
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Error 0.400 for 6/4, a 9/1 vs 8/2 comparison printout, error 0.300 for 7/3, and a three-node comparison table.
